title: Exporting full subitem rows to excel
excerpt:
Hi there, 
Is there a way to export items with subitems so that all the rows are visible in excel, not just the items? When I do an export with subitems, I just get the names of the subitems grouped into a single column …

posts.author: MichelleGilders (Michelle Gilders)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi there,
Is there a way to export items with subitems so that all the rows are visible in excel, not just the items? When I do an export with subitems, I just get the names of the subitems grouped into a single column but without each row with data on each subitem.
Thanks for your help
Michelle

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @MichelleGilders!

If you select the option to include subitems in the export, they should appear below the parent item - as shown in the screenshot below:

Screen Shot 2023-02-23 at 12.49.10 pm1260×198 22.6 KB

Is this not the case for you?

I did also want to mention that our team are working on improving the layout of the export - primarily that second column - subitems - next to the item name. We have received feedback that it just become messy and unnecessary - feel free to share your feedback too!

posts.author: MichelleGilders (Michelle Gilders)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thank you! My error was running test exports with only the title of the subitem and no content in the rows. All working correctly now,

Kind regards,

Michelle

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml: Thanks for the update! Glad to hear this is working for you now

posts.author: Iryna_Belaya (Iryna Belaya)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello @MichelleGilders ,
If you’re open to the apps, you can test Smart Spreadsheet for monday as it auto-imports subitems into a spreadsheet on a separate tab.
